This classification places Zephyranthes jamesonii among other similar plants such as the lily and the daffodil. The genus Zephyranthes is part of the order Asparagales, a group of monocotyledonous flowering plants. The classification of Zephyranthes jamesonii reflects its botanical relationships and evolutionary history.

intro: Zephyranthes jamesonii, commonly known as the Jameson's Zephyr Lily, is a species of flowering plant in the family Amaryllidaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its purported health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Zephyranthes jamesonii include alkaloids such as jamesonine, which is believed to contribute to the plant's medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Zephyranthes jamesonii is often prepared as a tincture or infused in tea form to take advantage of its health benefits, although its use should be carefully monitored due to potential toxicity and interactions with other medications.
